iterate_latest.summary: Fix iterate following ADR-131 / PR #110 (diagnosis). Adds buffer-aware routing in attachTouchScroll: a new routeScroll(term, lines, sendData) helper reads term.buffer.active.type and branches — alt-buffer emits Cursor-Up/Down keystrokes via the optional sendData callback (the TUI scrolls itself); normal-buffer keeps term.scrollLines(lines). EmbeddedTerminal.tsx:215 wires sendData to (payload) => socket.send({type:'data', payload}), the same WS data path term.onData uses. Three alt-buffer bench assertions from PR #110 invert into regression guards; two new ones cover normal-buffer preservation and absent-sendData defense-in-depth. Production code change ~15 LOC. iPad UAT post-deploy is the final empirical gate.

server.reason: client-only diff (no server/** file touched); touch-scroll is a pure client module

unit.evidence: full client vitest 149 files / 1556 tests green (PR #110 baseline 1553 → 1556 = +3 net). touch-scroll cohort 21/21 green (15 mock + 6 alt-buffer). tsc --noEmit clean; oxlint clean on changed files. File sizes after change: touch-scroll.ts 211 LOC, touch-scroll.test.ts 204 LOC, touch-scroll.alt-buffer.test.ts 189 LOC — all under the 300-LOC ceiling. EmbeddedTerminal.tsx wire-up is 3 LOC + 6-line comment block.

integration.reason: test-only at the unit boundary; the integration through socket.send re-uses the term.onData path which is exercised by every existing terminal integration test

e2e.reason: Phase Matrix at small: E2E required if feature+UI OR touches_io_boundary; this is BUG with no risk flag. Bench-cohort (4 alt-buffer assertions with real @xterm/xterm) covers the routing semantics empirically. EmbeddedTerminal wire-up reuses the already test-covered socket.send path. iPad UAT post-deploy is the final user-facing gate.

surface_verification.justification: Small BUG-fix to touch-scroll routing. Production change is ~15 LOC: a 12-LOC routeScroll helper that reads term.buffer.active.type and branches, plus a 3-LOC EmbeddedTerminal.tsx wire-up that passes socket.send as the sendData callback. The routing semantics are empirically covered by 4 new alt-buffer tests using a real @xterm/xterm Terminal in jsdom. The EmbeddedTerminal wire-up is mechanical reuse of an already test-covered path. Real iPad UAT is the final gate post-deploy, not a CI-runnable surface here.

behaviors.behavior: alt-buffer downward pan: scrollLines is NOT called; sendData receives Cursor-Down (\x1b[B) × line count
behaviors.evidence: touch-scroll.alt-buffer.test.ts 'alt-buffer: scrollLines is NOT called; sendData receives Cursor-Down escape × line count (downward pan)' — joined sendData calls assert .toBe('\x1b[B'.repeat(5))

behaviors.behavior: alt-buffer upward pan: scrollLines is NOT called; sendData receives Cursor-Up (\x1b[A) × |line count|
behaviors.evidence: touch-scroll.alt-buffer.test.ts 'alt-buffer: upward pan emits Cursor-Up escape × |line count|' — symmetric to the down case

behaviors.behavior: normal-buffer pan preserves PR #61 behavior: scrollLines IS called with the line count; sendData is NOT invoked
behaviors.evidence: touch-scroll.alt-buffer.test.ts 'normal-buffer: scrollLines IS called; sendData is NOT invoked (preserves pre-iterate behavior)'

behaviors.behavior: absent sendData in alt-buffer is a clean no-op (no throw, no scrollLines fallback — defense-in-depth)
behaviors.evidence: touch-scroll.alt-buffer.test.ts 'absent sendData callback in alt-buffer is a clean no-op (defensive — no throw, no scrollLines fallback)'

behaviors.behavior: REGRESSION: xterm.js DECSET 1049 flips buffer.active.type to 'alternate' (unchanged by this iterate)
behaviors.evidence: touch-scroll.alt-buffer.test.ts 'real xterm: DECSET 1049 flips buffer.active.type to alternate' — preserved from PR #110 unchanged

behaviors.behavior: REGRESSION: scrollLines moves viewportY in normal-buffer but not in alt-buffer (unchanged by this iterate)
behaviors.evidence: touch-scroll.alt-buffer.test.ts 'real xterm: in the alt-buffer, scrollLines does NOT move viewportY' — preserved from PR #110 unchanged

behaviors.behavior: REGRESSION: the 15 mock-Terminal touch-scroll tests (consumeTouchDelta math + handler integration) still pass after the routing change
behaviors.evidence: touch-scroll.test.ts 15/15 green; mock Terminal extended with buffer.active.type='normal' so the router resolves cleanly; the accumulator + multi-touch + dispose + getPixelsPerLine seam tests are unchanged in shape

behaviors.behavior: USER-FACING: a one-finger pan inside Claude's TUI moves the visible Claude transcript view on an iPad
behaviors.evidence: no CI surface can simulate Claude Code's TUI receiving arrow-key escapes and re-rendering with viewport motion; the bench cohort proves the routing semantics (what arrives at the pty), the iPad UAT proves the user-visible outcome

enumeration_basis.plus: BUG fix (Spec Impact NONE, no formal ACs) — basis = the 4 routing behaviors (down-pan + up-pan + normal-preserve + absent-callback no-op) + 2 PR #110 regression guards preserved + 1 mock-cohort regression guarantee + 1 user-facing iPad UAT (untestable, requires physical device).
